description: Declarative manifest of optional features in the Orbit SaaS starter kit. The generator CLI reads this to know which files to delete and which fenced regions to strip when a user opts out of a feature.

notes: Fences live inline in source files and are removed by the generator.
The strip engine auto-walks the whole repo looking for fences — the `fencedRegions` arrays below are advisory documentation only, not a whitelist.
Three fence syntaxes are accepted so markers work in TS/TSX, Prisma, and JSX: `// +feature:<name>`, `/* +feature:<name> */`, `{/* +feature:<name> */}`.

teams.name: teams
teams.label: Teams + per-team PBAC
teams.description: A second PBAC scope nested inside a workspace. Users can be grouped into teams and granted team-scoped roles (TEAM_ADMIN / TEAM_MEMBER + custom).

billing.name: billing
billing.label: Billing (subscriptions + webhooks)
billing.description: Subscriptions via a `BillingProvider` port. Ships with Stripe, Polar, and Dodo Payments adapters. Webhooks are signature-verified per provider. When disabled, `BILLING_PROVIDER` env goes away and `/billing/*` routes are removed.

provider.label: Which billing provider?
provider.notes: Choosing one provider enables its `billing-<provider>` sub-feature and strips the others. Pick 'stripe' for US-first SaaS, 'polar' if you want merchant-of-record + indie-friendly pricing, 'dodo' if you want merchant-of-record in a Stainless-generated SDK.

billing-stripe.name: billing-stripe
billing-stripe.label: Billing: Stripe adapter
billing-stripe.description: Stripe adapter for the `BillingProvider` port (checkout, customer portal, subscription webhooks). Enabled by default when `billing` is on; the CLI translates the chosen provider into this sub-feature. Requires the parent `billing` feature.

billing-polar.name: billing-polar
billing-polar.label: Billing: Polar adapter
billing-polar.description: Polar.sh adapter for the `BillingProvider` port (hosted checkout, customer sessions, Standard-Webhooks subscription events). Requires the parent `billing` feature. Polar is merchant-of-record.

billing-dodo.name: billing-dodo
billing-dodo.label: Billing: Dodo Payments adapter
billing-dodo.description: Dodo Payments adapter for the `BillingProvider` port (checkout sessions, customer portal, Standard-Webhooks subscription events). Requires the parent `billing` feature. Dodo is merchant-of-record with a Stainless-generated TypeScript SDK.

uploads.name: uploads
uploads.label: File uploads (UploadThing)
uploads.description: File storage via UploadThing (avatars, images). When off, the `/uploads/*` routes are removed and `fileStorage` falls back to a noop.

waitlist.name: waitlist
waitlist.label: Waitlist / private beta
waitlist.description: A private-beta gating flow: public can request access, only admin-accepted emails can sign up. When off, the waitlist table and routes are removed and `IS_WAITLIST_ENABLED` is hardcoded false. Requires `auth-magic-link` because the post-acceptance flow sends a magic link to complete sign-in.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.label: Realtime (WebSocket + presence)
realtime.description: In-process pub-sub hub, presence tracker, event publisher, and `/v1/ws` WebSocket endpoint. When off, the whole realtime stack is removed: no live member updates, no typing/presence indicators, no `connectRealtime` client.

email-resend.name: email-resend
email-resend.label: Resend email adapter
email-resend.description: Transactional emails (magic links, workspace invites) via Resend + react-email. When off, a `ConsoleMailer` logs emails to stdout — useful for local dev or SaaS variants that ship without SMTP configured.

jobs.name: jobs
jobs.label: Background jobs + cron
jobs.description: Provider-neutral `JobQueue` + `JobRuntime` ports. Services enqueue work without knowing which adapter is wired underneath. Ships with graphile-worker (Postgres-backed) and Upstash QStash (managed HTTP) adapters. When disabled, services that would have enqueued work must run inline and no worker process boots.

provider.label: Which jobs provider?
provider.notes: Choosing a provider enables its `jobs-<provider>` sub-feature and strips the others. Pick 'graphile' for self-hosted Postgres-backed queues with cron; pick 'qstash' for a managed HTTP queue that deploys well on Vercel / Cloudflare Workers.

jobs-graphile.name: jobs-graphile
jobs-graphile.label: Jobs: graphile-worker adapter
jobs-graphile.description: graphile-worker adapter for the `JobQueue` + `JobRuntime` ports. Postgres-backed queue with `LISTEN`/`NOTIFY` dispatch, crontab scheduling, retries with exponential backoff, and a `graphile_worker.jobs` observability table. Requires the parent `jobs` feature.

rate-limit.name: rate-limit
rate-limit.label: Rate limiting
rate-limit.description: A `RateLimiter` port with in-memory (dev) and Upstash Redis (prod) adapters, plus a Hono middleware that guards the auth + waitlist surface with per-IP and per-email limiters. When disabled, the port, middleware, adapters, and limiter wiring on routes are all removed — including the demo-start limiter, which uses the same port.

provider.label: Which rate-limit provider?
provider.notes: Choosing a provider enables its `rate-limit-<provider>` sub-feature and strips the others. Pick 'memory' for dev + single-instance deploys (state is not shared across workers). Pick 'upstash' for production with self-hosted infra (Redis-backed, generic). Pick 'unkey' if you also want per-API-key quotas and a managed dashboard — Unkey positions itself as a global rate-limiting + API-key product.

rate-limit-upstash.name: rate-limit-upstash
rate-limit-upstash.label: Rate limiting: Upstash adapter
rate-limit-upstash.description: Upstash Redis adapter for the `RateLimiter` port. Uses `@upstash/ratelimit`'s sliding-window algorithm with a shared `Redis` client; fails open on Redis error so an Upstash outage cannot take the auth surface down. Requires the parent `rate-limit` feature.

rate-limit-unkey.name: rate-limit-unkey
rate-limit-unkey.label: Rate limiting: Unkey adapter
rate-limit-unkey.description: Unkey adapter for the `RateLimiter` port. Uses `@unkey/ratelimit` with a namespace per `(limit, duration)` shape; fails open on network error so an Unkey outage cannot take the auth surface down. Pair this with Unkey's API-key product when you ship public API keys. Requires the parent `rate-limit` feature.

jobs-qstash.name: jobs-qstash
jobs-qstash.label: Jobs: Upstash QStash adapter
jobs-qstash.description: Upstash QStash adapter for the `JobQueue` + `JobRuntime` ports plus a `JobDispatcher` that runs inside the `/v1/jobs/run/:name` HTTP route. No worker process — QStash posts back to this API, so the kit deploys cleanly on serverless runtimes (Vercel, Cloudflare Workers). Requires the parent `jobs` feature.

frontend.name: frontend
frontend.label: Frontend (web app)
frontend.description: The authenticated web app. Ships two sibling implementations — `apps/web-tanstack` (TanStack Start + Vite) and `apps/web-next` (Next 16 App Router). They hit the same Hono API and share the same `@orbit/ui` primitives + `@orbit/shared` DTOs, but the per-page code is intentionally duplicated so each uses its framework idiomatically. Pick one; the CLI deletes the other.

framework.label: Which web framework?
framework.notes: Choosing a framework enables its `frontend-<framework>` sub-feature and strips the other. Pick 'tanstack' for TanStack Start + Vite + TanStack Router (typed file-based routing, great DX, SSR-ready). Pick 'next' for Next 16 App Router (mainstream ecosystem, easiest deploy on Vercel).

frontend-tanstack.name: frontend-tanstack
frontend-tanstack.label: Frontend: TanStack Start app
frontend-tanstack.description: The `apps/web-tanstack` app (TanStack Start + Vite, port 4001). Enabled by default when `frontend` is on; the CLI translates the chosen framework into this sub-feature. Requires the parent `frontend` feature. When disabled, the entire `apps/web-tanstack/` folder is deleted and the `dev:web` root script is repointed at the surviving sibling.

frontend-next.name: frontend-next
frontend-next.label: Frontend: Next 16 App Router app
frontend-next.description: The `apps/web-next` app (Next 16 App Router, port 4003). Requires the parent `frontend` feature. When disabled, the entire `apps/web-next/` folder is deleted.

auth-magic-link.name: auth-magic-link
auth-magic-link.label: Magic-link auth (passwordless email)
auth-magic-link.description: Passwordless sign-in via better-auth's `magicLink` plugin. The server sends a one-time link through the `Mailer` port; in dev the last-sent link is cached and exposed at `/v1/dev/last-magic-link` so you can click through without an inbox. When off, the plugin, the dev endpoint, the client plugin, the `MagicLinkEmail` mailer method, and the magic-link form on `/login` are all removed — at least one of `auth-magic-link` or `auth-password` must be enabled for sign-in to work.

auth-password.name: auth-password
auth-password.label: Email + password auth
auth-password.description: Email-and-password sign-in and sign-up via better-auth's built-in `emailAndPassword` strategy. Adds a password form on `/login` (with a signin/signup toggle) alongside any other auth methods. Password hashes are stored on the existing `accounts.password` column — no schema change required. When off, the config flag and the password form are both removed. At least one of `auth-password` or `auth-magic-link` must be enabled.

auth-admin.name: auth-admin
auth-admin.label: App-level admin (better-auth admin plugin)
auth-admin.description: Enables the better-auth `admin` plugin — adds app-wide `role` (admin/user), `banned`, `banReason`, `banExpires` columns on users and an `impersonatedBy` column on sessions, plus the admin endpoints (listUsers, setRole, banUser, impersonate). In dev, a `/v1/dev/make-admin` endpoint lets the current user self-promote so the plugin is usable without manual DB edits. When off, the plugin and its columns are removed.

demo.name: demo
demo.label: Demo mode (Orbit marketing site only)
demo.description: The self-expiring demo workspace behind `POST /v1/demo/start` on the Orbit marketing deploy. Not shipped to customers: the CLI always strips it, and the public starter build does too (it's tagged `paid` so `scripts/build-starter.ts` removes it). Present in the monorepo because wereorbit.com itself boots with DEMO_ENABLED=true so prospects can click through a hydrated workspace without signing up.

orm.name: orm
orm.label: ORM
orm.description: Database access layer. The parent feature is always on (every project needs an ORM). The provider select picks exactly ONE of the sub-features — `orm-prisma` (free, default) or `orm-drizzle` (paid). Selecting one strips the other.

provider.label: Which ORM?
provider.notes: Pick 'prisma' for the default free track — DMMF-driven client, excellent type safety, great migrations story. Pick 'drizzle' (paid) for a SQL-first API, smaller runtime, and composable query builders. When 'drizzle' is selected, the Prisma schema stays the source of truth; a generator emits the Drizzle schema on `npm run prisma:generate` so columns are only defined once.

orm-prisma.name: orm-prisma
orm-prisma.label: ORM: Prisma adapter
orm-prisma.description: Prisma ORM track. Ships `apps/api/prisma/` + generated client + `Prisma*Repository` implementations + `PrismaUnitOfWork`. Auth uses `@better-auth/prisma-adapter`. Enabled by default when `orm.provider=prisma`.

orm-drizzle.name: orm-drizzle
orm-drizzle.label: ORM: Drizzle adapter
orm-drizzle.description: Drizzle ORM track. Ships `apps/api/drizzle/` (drizzle-kit config + migrations) + generated Drizzle schema + `Drizzle*Repository` implementations + `DrizzleUnitOfWork`. Auth uses `@better-auth/drizzle-adapter`. The Prisma schema remains the source of truth — `prisma-generator-drizzle` emits the Drizzle schema on `npm run prisma:generate`. Migrations are managed with drizzle-kit (`npm run drizzle:generate`, `npm run drizzle:migrate`). Requires the parent `orm` feature.

audit-log.name: audit-log
audit-log.label: Audit log
audit-log.description: Append-only audit trail at two scopes. `AppAuditEntry` is the admin-facing global ledger for moderation-class events (bans, impersonations). `WorkspaceAuditEntry` is tenant-scoped, read by workspace and team admins, and optionally narrowed to a team via `teamId`. Entries are materialised by a post-commit projector listening to the domain event bus, so services never write audit rows directly. Adds `workspace.audit_log.view`, `workspace.audit_log.export`, and (when teams is on) `team.audit_log.view` permissions.

cli-master.name: cli-master
cli-master.label: CLI maintainer-only assets
cli-master.description: Skills and tooling that only make sense in the master Orbit template repo, not in scaffolded customer projects. Includes the `add-feature-fence`, `prisma-drizzle-sync`, and `add-paid-feature` skills — they document master-only concerns (fence markers, dual-ORM sync, declaring new paid features in features.json + PAID_FEATURES). Always stripped at scaffold time because `defaultEnabled` is false and the CLI never enables it. Pattern matches `demo`: tagged paid + default-off so both the private and public starter builds drop it.

audit-retention.name: audit-retention
audit-retention.label: Audit log retention (cron)
audit-retention.description: Daily cron job that deletes audit entries older than `AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS`. Requires both `audit-log` and `jobs` because the cleanup runs as a scheduled background job. Default retention is 90 days; set the env to 0 (or any non-positive value) to disable cleanup at runtime while keeping the schedule registered.

auth-oauth.name: auth-oauth
auth-oauth.label: OAuth providers (Google + Apple)
auth-oauth.description: Social sign-in via better-auth's `socialProviders`. When off, only magic-link auth remains — simpler UX, smaller env surface. Can be re-added later by restoring the fenced blocks.

notes: When a feature is disabled, the CLI:
2. Walks the repo once and removes every line between `// +feature:<name>` and `// -feature:<name>` (inclusive). The walk skips node_modules, .git, dist, .turbo, .next, etc.
3. Removes the env keys listed in `features.<name>.envKeys` from every `apps/*/.env.example`.
The fence format is matched by a per-line regex (`^\s*//\s*\+feature:<name>\s*$` and equivalents for `/* */` and `{/* */}`). Nested DIFFERENT-name fences are allowed; nested same-name fences throw.
